arabic (native) russian (low) french (low) research projects heinkel wespe project wasp was an attempt to challenge maneuverability, structure, conventional takeoff, and thrust efficiency. in this project the outcome was to functionally have an aircraft with a shroud capable of having a vertical takeoff from an upright position and maintaining its flight path without crashing. additionally, it should return to a horizontal position to fly in a normal path and perform maneuverable moves objectives design and specify the wasps size, materials used, tools, motor, and dimensions. produce an autocad design for the construction parts of the wasp build a prototype of the wasp based on designed specifications execute test on the produced prototype dual/hybrid thrust reverser mechanism the purpose of this project was to integrate two reverse thrust systems (cold stream and bucket type) in one mechanism, thus achieving the maximum amount of reverse thrust produced when deployed. as a result, the fuel consumption, as well as the distance needed to stop the aircraft will decrease. objectives design an autocad design of the dual/hybrid system the system should be electrically remote controlled two systems opening sequentially deployment of bucket type individually the hummingbird based on the foregoing discussion, the mechanical problem of aerobatic flight has been reconsidered and a new configuration has been devised. the solution presented in this project promises to enhance performance on the vertical dimension through higher thrust-to- weight ratios (thus reducing the need for high speeds), while retaining a small, light, agile aircraft. a propeller shroud (duct) is to be incorporated, along with counter-rotating propellers and engines. the duct is to be configured to function as an annular lifting surface and as a wing brace. objectives produce a 3d model of the hummingbird the aircraft is to be aerodynamically symmetrical. to minimize mass inertias, all heavy or rotating components (engines, propellers) are to be located near the aircraft center of gravity for control (with power) at low and zero airspeed, tail surfaces are to be located in close proximity to the propellers and centrally in the propellers wash
